steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence announces "three types of muscle" and then lists smooth, cardiac, and skeletal. The blank must introduce that list with correct punctuation after a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — tissue and smooth,: the construction before the list is not a complete independent clause, so the punctuation is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — tissue: smooth,: a colon correctly follows the independent clause and introduces the three-item list. ✓
  • Choice C — tissue smooth,: omitting punctuation runs the list into the clause without a clear separator. ✗
  • Choice D — tissue. Smooth: splits the sentence so the list becomes a fragment disconnected from the clause that introduces it. ✗
That matches Choice B — tissue: smooth,.
